title: House Robber
description:
You are a professional robber planning to rob houses along a street. Each house has a certain amount of money stashed. The only constraint stopping you from robbing each of them is that adjacent houses have security systems connected — if two adjacent houses are broken into on the same night, the police will be triggered.

Given an integer array `nums` representing the amount of money in each house, return the maximum amount of money you can rob tonight without alerting the police.

parts.prompt: Given an array of house values, find the maximum you can rob without robbing two adjacent houses.
parts.internalNotes: Classic 1D DP. dp[i] = max(dp[i-1], dp[i-2] + nums[i]). Can optimize to O(1) space with two variables. Common mistakes: not considering the skip case, off-by-one at boundaries.

hints.content: For each house, you have two choices. Think about what optimal means at each step.
This is a dynamic programming problem. What's the recurrence relation?
dp[i] = max(dp[i-1], dp[i-2] + nums[i]). The choice at each house: skip it (take dp[i-1]) or rob it (dp[i-2] + nums[i]).

followUps: Can you do this with O(1) space?
parts.expectedApproach: 1D DP with recurrence dp[i] = max(dp[i-1], dp[i-2] + nums[i]). Optimize to two variables.

transitionCriteria.transitionPrompt: Now what if the houses are arranged in a circle? The first and last house are adjacent.

parts.prompt: The houses are arranged in a circle — the first and last houses are adjacent. Same rules: can't rob adjacent houses. Find the maximum amount.
parts.internalNotes: Key insight: can't rob both house 0 and house n-1. Run the linear DP twice: once on houses [0..n-2], once on [1..n-1], take the max. Common mistake: trying to add a circular constraint to the DP directly.

hints.content: The circular constraint means you can't take both the first and last house.
What if you solved two separate linear problems?
Run house robber on nums[0..n-2] and nums[1..n-1]. Take the max of both results.

testCases.explanation: Can't rob both house 0 and 2, best is just house 1
Rob house 1 and 3: 2+1=3, or house 0 and 2: 1+3=4

followUps: What's the time and space complexity?
Could you extend this to a tree structure (binary tree of houses)?
parts.expectedApproach: Two passes of linear house robber: [0..n-2] and [1..n-1], take max.
